scenario: Beacon Bank models a Camunda 8.8 transaction routing workflow that handles 50000 transactions per day. The team checks which BPMN gateway icons the coverage page lists with an implementation marker before locking down the modeling palette for downstream teams.
question: Which gateway icon in the BPMN coverage page is shown without an implementation marker in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The complex gateway icon on the BPMN coverage gateways section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The exclusive gateway icon on the BPMN coverage gateways section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The parallel gateway icon on the BPMN coverage gateways section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The inclusive gateway icon on the BPMN coverage gateways section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8

a.text: Correct. The BPMN coverage page lists the complex gateway icon without an implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 does not execute complex gateways at the engine level.

b.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the exclusive gateway icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes exclusive gateways for data-based routing.

c.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the parallel gateway icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes parallel gateways for fork and join semantics.

d.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the inclusive gateway icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes inclusive gateways with conditional outgoing flows.

explanation: The BPMN coverage gateways section marks exclusive, parallel, event-based, and inclusive gateways as implemented, while the complex gateway icon appears without an implementation marker.
